evals.name: easy-mode-order-with-instantly-upload
evals.prompt: Order 9 mailboxes for Consulti AI (https://consulti.ai) via Inbox Insiders. Sender name 'Jay Feldman'. Connect them to Instantly automatically. Show exact API call.

expectations.text: Quotes the dollar total before calling /instant-orders (e.g., $31.50/mo recurring + $36 one-time = $67 first invoice)
Uses POST https://inboxinsiders.io/api/v1/instant-orders endpoint
Authorization header is Bearer ${INBOX_INSIDERS_API_KEY} format
Payload mode is 'easy'
Payload quantity is 9
Payload sender_name is 'Jay Feldman' (first + last)
cold_email is a NESTED object with provider and api_key (not flat top-level cold_email_provider)
cold_email.provider is 'instantly'
Includes idempotency_key (uuid v4) in the payload
Mentions Stripe charge fires immediately on POST
No placeholder text remains (TODO, REPLACE, TBD)

evals.name: full-control-mode-existing-domains
evals.prompt: I already own acme.com, foo.com, and bar.com. Use Inbox Insiders to provision mailboxes on them and connect to Instantly. Sender 'Madison Popoff'.

expectations.text: Uses mode 'full_control' in the payload
Payload includes domains array [acme.com, foo.com, bar.com]
Does NOT include quantity field (full_control uses domains array, not quantity)
Mentions full_control skips the $12/domain registration charge
sender_name is 'Madison Popoff' (first + last)
Includes idempotency_key
cold_email is nested with provider 'instantly' and api_key

evals.name: poll-order-status
evals.prompt: I just got run_id 7c4f2e6a-1b3d-4f5e-9a8b-2c3d4e5f6a7b from Inbox Insiders. How do I check if the order is done?

expectations.text: Uses GET https://inboxinsiders.io/api/v1/instant-orders?run_id={uuid} endpoint
Polling cadence is 60s (not faster than 30s)
Lists terminal statuses: completed, completed_with_warnings, failed
Lists non-terminal statuses (pending, pending_payment, pending_setup, running) that mean keep polling
Mentions typical run time of 5-30 minutes (and 5-10 extra for Instantly upload)
Authorization Bearer header is included

evals.name: export-credentials-gating
evals.prompt: My order with run_id abc-123 just hit status 'pending_setup'. Can I export the credentials now?

expectations.text: Says NO — export only works once status is terminal (completed or completed_with_warnings)
Recommends polling GET /instant-orders?run_id=... until terminal status first
Mentions the export endpoint GET /instant-orders/export?run_id={uuid} for when status is terminal
Treats credentials as plaintext secrets (warns against logging or committing)
Refuses or flags that 'Jay' alone will be rejected — sender_name requires first AND last name
Asks for or suggests a canonical full name like 'Jay Feldman'
References standard senders (Jay Feldman, Madison Popoff, Bob Porter) as canonical options
Does NOT call POST /instant-orders with the invalid sender_name

evals.name: stripe-charge-confirmation-gate
evals.prompt: Place an order for 12 mailboxes for brand 'TestCo' right now.

expectations.text: Quotes the dollar total before calling create order: 12 × $3.50/mo = $42/mo recurring + 4 × $12 = $48 one-time
Asks for explicit user confirmation before calling POST /instant-orders (does not auto-execute)
Mentions that POST /instant-orders charges Stripe IMMEDIATELY
Generates idempotency_key (uuid v4) to include in the payload

evals.name: idempotency-on-retry
evals.prompt: I called POST /instant-orders and got a 500 error. I'm not sure if the charge went through. What do I do?

expectations.text: Says to retry with the SAME idempotency_key (not a new one) to avoid duplicate charges
Mentions the server caches the response for that idempotency_key (including the original error/success)
Recommends checking the II dashboard or polling for an existing run_id before assuming the charge failed
Warns NOT to rotate the idempotency_key until duplicate-charge risk is ruled out
